content.full_text:
October 22, 1976

Mr. Henry J. Kroeger
Republican Associates
555 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Kroeger:

Thank you for your letter of October 4, inquiring as to whether
Republican Associates is a committee as defined by the Political
Reform Act and must file campaign statements accordingly.

You have stated that the Republican Associates is not an official
committee of the Republican party and does not contribute funds
to candidates or committees or make expenditures in support of
candidates or committees. The Associates is, therefore, not a
committee under the law and has no obligation to file campaign
statements.

I trust the above answers your question, however, if you desire
additional information, please let me know.

Very truly yours,


REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator


RSK:mak
bcc: Jeanne

Republican Associates
555 Capitol Mall·Sacramento,California 95814·443-7474


October 4, 1976


Commissioners
Fair Political Practices
Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Commissioners:

In reviewing your commission ruling of April 22, 1976, the members of
Republican Associates feel that their organization is not a committee as
defined under G.C. 82025. The purpose of this letter is to solicit your
opinion.

A description of the Republican Associates organization follows:

Membership:    Republican Associates membership fluctuates between
               150-250 individuals, including Independents and
               Democrats, from the greater Sacramento area.

Finance:       Republican Associates receives 95% of its financing
               from dues, 5% from luncheon and reception profits.

Expenses:      Republican Associates expends 75% of its monies to
               salary a part time executive director, 20% to fund
               a membership newsletter and 5% for miscellaneous
               expenses.

Purpose:       Republican Associates keeps its membership abreast
               of political events through its newsletters and flyers;
               brings interesting speakers before the R/A members and
               their guests; gives candidates and campaigns information
               about Sacramento County; and conducts periodic education
               classes about the Republican Party.

Misc:          1. Republican Associates does not contribute money to
                  candidates. R/A does sponsor candidates' nights and has
                  in the past allowed each candidate to keep the profits
                  from tickets their campaign members have sold. The
                  largest of these events occurred in 1975. The highest
                  profit made by one candidate was $110.00.

               2. Equipment and facilities used by Republican Associates
                  are donated. Current equipment consists of one typewriter
                  and two file cabinets and two desks with chairs.

Republican Associates
Letter to FFTC
October 4, 1976
Page 2

3. The current office space occupied by Republican
Associates is an incomplete property within the
555 Capitol Mall structure. It consists of cement floors
and the lack of interior walls. Republican Associates
has an understanding with the property owners to vacate
when a tenant is found. In fact, R/A/ will vacate the
555 property within 30 days. Republican Associates does
maintain an answering service since the executive director
only works part time.

4. Republican Associates is not an official organization
of the Republican Party.

Republican Associates did not file Form 420, September 23, 1976, because it
was believed that it was not required under the interpretation of G.C. 82025.
If you have any question, please contact me at 443-7474.

Sincerely,

Henry J. Kroeger
Treasurer
Republican Associates
